hypothesis: When one modality is systematically absent or corrupted during inference (e.g., XRD patterns are unavailable for novel synthesized materials), X-MoPA's cross-modal predictive training will enable it to reconstruct a high-fidelity surrogate latent representation of the missing modality from the remaining two, such that property prediction accuracy degrades by less than 10% compared to the full three-modality setting, because the joint latent space has been explicitly trained to encode each modality as a deterministic function of the other two, making the framework inherently robust to real-world modality dropout in a way that simple concatenation-based multimodal models are not.
